item.title: Jesse Jackson, Civil Rights Leader Who Sought the Presidency, Dies at 84
item.description: An impassioned orator, he was a moral and political force who formed a “rainbow coalition” of poor and working-class people. His mission, he said, was...

item.title: Jesse Jackson: A Life in Pictures
item.description: The civil rights leader was one of the country’s most influential Black figures.

item.title: Freeze the Rent? Landlords Have Enough Trouble Already.
item.description: One in 10 buildings with rent-regulated apartments is losing money.

item.title: Ukraine and Russia Hold New Round of Peace Talks, but Expectations Are Low
item.description: Both sides described previous U.S.-mediated negotiations as productive, although they did not appear to address sticking points like territory and sec...

item.title: Tom Pritzker, Hyatt Heir, Steps Down as Executive Chairman Over Links to Epstein
item.description: The member of a prominent and wealthy family, Mr. Pritzker was in regular contact with the financier and sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.
